title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Https Identity
summary: A universal browser-and-Node library must state what HTTPS trust it can configure in each runtime and where proxies or mTLS require Node boundaries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts https identity increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Defend against decompression bombs, oversized structured values, deep nesting, and unbounded field counts. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
